generated_oml:
instance BuildingControlSystemSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Building Control System sensors modeled in BIM using Autodesk Revit with custom families and property sets"
    DTDFVocab:producedData BCSDataTransmitted
]

instance BCSDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom BuildingControlSystemSensor
]

instance BCSData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Sensor values collected at 1-second interval via heartbeat mechanism for state updates"
    DTDFVocab:fromData BCSDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseHono, EclipseDitto
]

instance LightingFixtureActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Lighting fixtures (IFCLIGHTFIXTURETYPE) with control capabilities defined via Revit metadata"
]

instance BIMModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Semantically enriched BIM models in IFC format as foundational geometric and semantic model"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ModelDrivenEngineering
]

instance EclipseHono : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Eclipse Hono for real-time data integration and at-least-once message delivery with persistent event storage"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataSynchronizationService, ModularServiceIntegrationService
]

instance EclipseDitto : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Eclipse Ditto for policy-based validation of sensor data and actuator command pre-execution checks"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataSynchronizationService, ModularServiceIntegrationService
]

instance ModelDrivenEngineering : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Model-Driven Engineering principles for automated generation of deployable DT artifacts from BIM models"
    DTDFVocab:enables ModelGenerationService
]

instance ModularArchitecture : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Modular architecture with standardized extension points for scalable service-oriented development"
    DTDFVocab:enables ModularServiceIntegrationService
]

instance DataSynchronizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuous data synchronization via Eclipse Hono and Ditto for real-time monitoring"
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeMonitoringInsight
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Interactive 3D visualization in Unreal Engine 5 for facility management oversight"
    DTDFVocab:provides VisualizationInsight, AdjustLightingCommand
]

instance ModelGenerationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Automated generation of deployable DT artifacts from BIM models using MDE"
    DTDFVocab:provides ModelBasedInsight
]

instance ModularServiceIntegrationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Extensible service modules for domain-specific analytics within UE5 visualization environments"
    DTDFVocab:provides ExtensibleAnalyticsInsight
]

instance RealTimeMonitoringInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time situational awareness of building systems"
]

instance VisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Interactive 3D visualization supporting fine-grained grouping and filtering of building components by storey"
]

instance AdjustLightingCommand : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Adjusting brightness and color temperature of lighting fixtures via DittoController"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance ModelBasedInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Semantically consistent geometric and behavioral representations for building components"
]

instance ExtensibleAnalyticsInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Extensible analytics capabilities through modular service integration"
]

instance BuildingInfrastructure : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "AECO domain building infrastructure operating across entire life cycle from design to operations"
    base:contains BuildingControlSystemSensor, LightingFixtureActuator
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "Control commands (e.g., actuator states, setpoint adjustments) are transmitted via Websocket to Eclipse Ditto, which routes them through Eclipse Hono to physical assets. Command execution is confirmed before updating the digital twin's state to ensure synchronization."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin employs a 1-second heartbeat mechanism for real-time synchronization between physical and digital components, as validated by the ODTE timeliness metric (0.997), ensuring minimal delay in state updates. This temporal granularity supports continuous data exchange with sub-second latency for operational monitoring and control, confirmed through bidirectional communication testing where physical state changes are reflected in the DT within the heartbeat interval."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The system architecture features a central DT Framework hub coordinating roles including Building Control Engineers (device modeling and deployment initiation), Facility Managers (real-time monitoring and control), and DT Engineers (custom service integration). Model-Driven Engineering (MDE) principles transform enriched BIM models into deployable artifacts, with modular extension points enabling scalable service-oriented development. Bidirectional data exchange is facilitated via Eclipse Hono and Ditto, ensuring holistic life cycle management from design to operations."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin lifecycle includes design-time modeling using Autodesk Revit to define custom families and property sets for BCS devices, followed by model checking (e.g., validation of Instance General UUID via IFC model checker), export to Industry Foundation Classes (IFC) format, automated deployment via model-driven transformation pipeline generating runtime artifacts, runtime operation with real-time data synchronization using Eclipse Hono and Ditto, interactive 3D visualization in Unreal Engine 5 (UE5), and evolution through modular service integration. Role-based governance involves Building Control Engineers initiating deployment, Facility Managers monitoring and controlling operations, and DT Engineers extending functionality via standardized interfaces."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Model fidelity and validity are validated using the Overall Digital Twin Entanglement (ODTE) metric, which combines timeliness (0.997), reliability (1.0), and availability (1.0) scores derived from a 1-second heartbeat mechanism over a 12-minute measurement window. Validation includes Eclipse Ditto's policy-based checks for sensor data value ranges and units, pre-execution command validation, and a dedicated IFC model checker to verify metadata completeness (e.g., Instance General UUID presence). Fault tolerance mechanisms such as Eclipse Hono's at-least-once delivery and Ditto's persistent event storage ensure resilience against network disruptions."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "Eclipse Hono and Ditto form the communication backbone using HTTP POST for initial data transfer and Websocket for real-time bidirectional communication. Network topology connects physical devices to Hono, then to Ditto, and finally to the UE5 visualization layer, with metadata-driven configuration for device endpoints."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "Automated deployment via model-driven framework using Hono/Ditto Configurator and Datasmith for UE5 conversion; modular architecture with standardized extension points supports scalability from individual components to entire infrastructure systems; runtime configuration management enables adjustments without full redeployment."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "Integration with external systems leverages Eclipse Hono and Ditto for real-time data synchronization, supporting interoperability across heterogeneous infrastructure systems through standardized BIM semantics and modular extension points for service-oriented integration with enterprise applications."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The framework adheres to Industry Foundation Classes (IFC) for BIM data exchange, as specified by buildingSMART standards, and integrates Eclipse Hono and Ditto for IoT communication, ensuring interoperability across heterogeneous BIM tools, IoT protocols, and visualization systems (NF1). It addresses fragmented deployments by standardizing data schemas through IFC and leveraging Eclipse IoT ecosystem components for bidirectional data flow, though specific protocol standards (e.g., MQTT) are not explicitly detailed beyond the use of Hono and Ditto."
]
